growth_records.medium: fermentation medium (succinic acid production) with Mg2(OH)2CO3 neutralizer/CO2 co-substrate

up.units: not biomass-normalized; consumed (glucose conversion yield 0.78 g succinate/g glucose reported)
up.method: glucose assay kit; conversion analyzed across systems

sec.method: HPLC; PEC-biohybrid at -0.3 V vs RHE, 24 h

sec.units: ~144 umol after 8 h (abiotic HER on photocathode, Faradaic eff. 48.3%)
~425.7 nmol after 3 h (abiotic CO2RR, Faradaic eff. 0.38%)

growth_records.cite: Feng, Tianhang et al. Photoelectrocatalytic-microbial biohybrid for succinic acid synthesis. Nature Communications (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41467-026-69962-4. [PMC13039688]
growth_records.snippet: The NiO@PAA@NHS/Au@A. succinogenes biohybrid system demonstrated superior performance, achieving a remarkable 67% CO2 conversion rate along with a glucose conversion yield of 0.78 g/g glucose... the PEC-biohybrid demonstrated the highest yi
growth_records.notes: NON-STANDARD growth condition: this is a photoelectrocatalytic-microbial biohybrid (electrode-immobilized cells, external electrons, CO2 co-substrate), not a conventional planktonic fermentation. Succinate production rate 1.41 g/L/h/cm2 is normalized to electrode area, NOT biomass (no gDW / OD / growth rate reported) -> cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h. Product yield = 0.78 g succinic acid / g glucose (biohybrid). Succinic acid = 57.18% of total product (Fig 3f). Byproducts acetic/formic/pyruvic acid detected but not quantified in text. Comparators: EC-biohybrid 0.75, PC-biohybrid 0.58, dark/open-circuit 0.21 g/L/h/cm2. Cells (WT and Au@A.succinogenes) resuspended to OD600 ~2.0 for immobilization. Strain 130Z ATCC 55618. Fermentation at 37 C, anaerobic, CO2-saturated.
